title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a school concert.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Is your daughter playing in the school concert? || Man: Yes, and she's excited. I thought the concert was on Thursday, but I was wrong. || Woman: When is it? || Man: [8]It's been moved to Wednesday because the hall is needed for exams on Thursday[/8]. I'm glad I checked the email, or I'd have missed it. || Woman: Well, I hope it works out, because it sounds as if you've thought about it carefully. || Man: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Is your daughter playing in the school concert?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, and she's excited. I thought the concert was on Thursday, but I was wrong.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: When is it?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It's been moved to Wednesday because the hall is needed for exams on Thursday. I'm glad I checked the email, or I'd have missed it.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, I hope it works out, because it sounds as if you've thought about it carefully.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time.

questions.question: Why is the concert on Wednesday?

options.A: The hall is not free on Thursday.
options.B: The man's daughter is busy on Thursday.
options.C: The school sent the wrong email.

questions.explanation: He says the hall is needed for exams on Thursday, so the concert was moved.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a new pair of shoes.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are those the shoes you ordered online? || Woman: Yes. They look just like the picture, and the size is right. || Man: So you're keeping them? || Woman: I think so, although [9]they feel a bit hard at the back, so I'll only wear them for short trips until they get softer[/9]. I expected them to be comfortable immediately. || Man: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are those the shoes you ordered online?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. They look just like the picture, and the size is right.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So you're keeping them?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I think so, although they feel a bit hard at the back, so I'll only wear them for short trips until they get softer. I expected them to be comfortable immediately.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the shoes?

options.B: They are not completely comfortable yet.
options.C: They look different from the online picture.

questions.explanation: She says they feel hard at the back and she will wear them only for short trips.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a museum café.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you eat at the museum café? || Woman: Yes. The sandwiches were fresh, but there weren't many choices. || Man: Was it expensive? || Woman: Not really. [10]What surprised me was how slow the queue was, even though there were only a few people in front of us[/10]. We nearly missed the start of the tour. || Woman: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful. || Man: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you eat at the museum café?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. The sandwiches were fresh, but there weren't many choices.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Was it expensive?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not really. What surprised me was how slow the queue was, even though there were only a few people in front of us. We nearly missed the start of the tour.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

questions.question: What surprised the woman about the café?
questions.explanation: She says the queue was slow even with only a few people ahead.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling a friend about an online course.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How's your online business course going? || Man: Pretty well. I thought studying from home might feel lonely, but there are group chats. || Woman: Are the videos useful? || Man: Yes, but [11]I learn most from the short tasks we have to complete after each video[/11]. They make me check that I've understood the ideas. || Man: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later. || Woman: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How's your online business course going?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Pretty well. I thought studying from home might feel lonely, but there are group chats.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are the videos useful?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but I learn most from the short tasks we have to complete after each video. They make me check that I've understood the ideas.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

questions.question: What helps the man learn most on the course?
questions.explanation: He says he learns most from the short tasks after each video.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a town festival.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you going to the town festival this year? || Woman: Probably. Last year the music was good, but the food stalls were all in one narrow street. || Man: Has that changed? || Woman: Yes, [12]they're putting the stalls around the square this time, so people won't have to push through crowds to buy lunch[/12]. That should improve things. || Woman: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think. || Man: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you going to the town festival this year?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Probably. Last year the music was good, but the food stalls were all in one narrow street.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Has that changed?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, they're putting the stalls around the square this time, so people won't have to push through crowds to buy lunch. That should improve things.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

questions.question: What change does the woman mention?

options.A: There will be more music events.
options.B: Food stalls will be in a better place.

questions.explanation: She says the stalls are being put around the square to reduce crowds.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a guided city tour.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: The guided tour was interesting, especially the old market area. The guide knew a lot, and she answered questions clearly. But I wouldn't recommend it for families with young children. [13]We stood in the same places for a long time while she explained the history, so some children in the group became bored[/13]. Adults who like history would enjoy it more. || Speaker: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: The guided tour was interesting, especially the old market area. The guide knew a lot, and she answered questions clearly. But I wouldn't recommend it for families with young children. We stood in the same places for a long time while she explained the history, so some children in the group became bored. Adults who like history would enjoy it more.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

questions.question: Why might the tour not suit young children?

options.A: The guide does not answer questions.
options.B: The market area is too crowded.
options.C: There is too much standing and listening.

questions.explanation: She says the group stood in the same places for a long time while the guide explained history.
